headline: Is Constellation Energy’s (CEG) DEI Push and Massive ESOP Shelf Quietly Redefining Its Capital Strategy?
summary: In March 2026, Constellation Energy filed a definitive proxy urging shareholders to vote against an investor proposal scrutinizing the return-on-investment of its diversity, equity, and inclusion programs, while also registering up to 50,000,000 common shares under a US$15.86 billion shelf tied to an employee stock ownership plan. These moves come as Constellation cements its role as the largest private U.S. power producer and a key nuclear supplier to data center giants under long-term...
